UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile information within the PaushtikPath application. This entity stores basic user details, assuming authentication is handled by an external system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the user profile.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was created.

FoodItem.title: FoodItem
FoodItem.description: Represents a specific food item with its nutritional breakdown. This entity forms the basis for calculating meal macros.

id.description: Unique identifier for the food item.

name.description: The common name of the food item, e.g., 'Dal Makhani' or 'Chapati'.

calories.description: Total calorie content per standard serving (e.g., 100g or 1 unit) of the food item.

protein.description: Protein content in grams per standard serving of the food item.

carbs.description: Carbohydrate content in grams per standard serving of the food item.

fats.description: Fat content in grams per standard serving of the food item.

MealLog.title: MealLog
MealLog.description: Represents a single instance of a user logging a specific food item with a given quantity at a particular time.

id.description: Unique identifier for the meal log entry.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who logged this meal. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N MealLog)

foodId.description: Reference to the FoodItem that was consumed. (Relationship: FoodItem 1:N MealLog)

quantity.description: The quantity of the food item consumed (e.g., in grams, units, or servings).

loggedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the meal was logged.

UserGoal.title: UserGoal
UserGoal.description: Represents the personalized daily nutritional goals set by a user, including calorie targets and macro distribution ratios.

id.description: Unique identifier for the user's nutritional goal configuration.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile to whom these goals belong. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:1 UserGoal)

targetCalories.description: The user's daily target calorie intake.

targetProteinRatio.description: The target percentage (as a decimal, e.g., 0.3 for 30%) of daily calories to come from protein.

targetCarbsRatio.description: The target percentage (as a decimal) of daily calories to come from carbohydrates.

targetFatsRatio.description: The target percentage (as a decimal) of daily calories to come from fats.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when these goals were last updated.

definition.description: Stores a user's basic profile information. Access is restricted to the authenticated user whose ID matches '{userId}' in the path. This is the root for all user-specific data.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user (from request.auth.uid).

definition.description: A master collection of generic Indian food items and their nutritional breakdowns. Documents are publicly readable by all users, supporting QAP-compliant list queries. Write access would typically be restricted to administrators or trusted backend services.

params.name: foodItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific food item.

definition.description: Stores individual meal log entries, private to the user specified by '{userId}'. Includes the denormalized 'userId' field within the document, which must match the path parameter for robust authorization independence and simple security rules. Supports QAP-compliant list queries for the owning user.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who logged this meal.

params.name: mealLogId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific meal log entry.

definition.description: Stores a user's personalized daily nutritional goals as a singleton document (with document ID 'userGoal') within their profile. This ensures private access to the user specified by '{userId}' and includes the denormalized 'userId' within the document, matching the path parameter for robust authorization independence and simple security rules.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this goal.

definition.description: A collection used for Database Access Control (DBAC) to designate administrators. The existence of a document at `/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid)` grants administrative privileges (e.g., to manage global food items).

params.name: uid
params.description: The unique identifier of a user who is designated as an administrator.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure is designed to be secure, scalable, and debuggable by strictly adhering to the core design principles. 

**1. Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**
*   For `MealLog` and `UserGoal` entities, which are intrinsically tied to a specific user, the `userId` is explicitly stored within each document. When these documents are placed in subcollections like `/userProfiles/{userId}/mealLogs/{mealLogId}` and `/userProfiles/{userId}/userGoal`, the `userId` in the document content *must* match the `{userId}` wildcard in the path. This denormalization eliminates the need for `get()` operations in security rules to check parent documents, thus ensuring authorization independence and enabling atomic batch/transactional writes.

**2. Structural Segregation for QAPs (Queryable Authorization Paths):**
*   **Public Data:** The `/foodItems` collection serves as a global, publicly readable catalog of food items. Its homogeneous security posture (all documents are public read, with admin-only write) means that `list` queries on this collection are inherently secure (QAP compliant) without needing complex filtering rules.
*   **Private User Data:** All user-specific data (`UserProfile`, `MealLog`, `UserGoal`) is strictly segregated under `/userProfiles/{userId}/...` paths. This path-based ownership ensures that a user can only query or modify their own data. For example, a `list` operation on `/userProfiles/$(request.auth.uid)/mealLogs` will securely return only the authenticated user's meal logs, fully satisfying QAPs.

**3. Consistent Access Modeling:**
*   **Path-Based Ownership:** This is the primary model for user-owned data. By embedding the `userId` directly into the document path (`/userProfiles/{userId}`), authorization rules become simple and explicit (e.g., `allow read, write: if request.auth.uid == userId`).
*   **Hierarchical Paths:** User-specific subcollections like `/userProfiles/{userId}/mealLogs` and `/userProfiles/{userId}/userGoal` continue the ownership hierarchy, making it clear that these resources belong to the parent user.
*   **DBAC for Global Roles:** An explicit `/roles_admin/{uid}` collection is included for Database Access Control. This allows for simple, boolean checks (e.g., `exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid))`) for privileged operations like creating/updating `FoodItem` entries, adhering to the principle of storing roles in the database rather than relying on custom claims.

**4. Data Clarity and Predictability:**
*   Semantic collection names (`userProfiles`, `foodItems`, `mealLogs`, `userGoal`, `roles_admin`) and descriptive wildcards (`{userId}`, `{foodItemId}`, `{mealLogId}`, `{uid}`) ensure that the purpose and ownership of each document are immediately clear, simplifying rule writing and debugging. The `userGoal` document is designed as a singleton under the user's path, clearly representing the 1:1 relationship with the user.
